content: 1 Peter 1:3-4: What inheritance does the new birth give us?
explanation: An eternal inheritance kept in heaven, which cannot perish or be defiled.
options: Gold and silver
Land
An inheritance incorruptible, undefiled, that does not fade away, kept in heaven
Power

content: 1 Peter 1:7: The testing of faith is compared to what?
explanation: Just as gold is refined in fire, faith is purified through trials.
options: Diamond
Precious stones
Iron and steel
Gold tested by fire (refined)

content: 1 Peter 2:9: What are believers called?
explanation: Every believer holds a noble status in the Lord: kings and priests.
options: Strangers
Common people
Servants
A chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, His own special people

content: 1 Peter 3:15: Always be ready to do what?
explanation: Be ready to explain your faith with meekness and reverence.
options: Give an answer to everyone who asks about the hope that is in you
Fight
Take revenge
Flee

content: 1 Peter 4:8: "Above all, have fervent ___ for one another, for love will cover a multitude of sins"
explanation: Love is the top priority in every relationship.
options: Fasting
Love for one another
Prayer
Obedience

content: 1 Peter 4:12: When you face trials for the Lord, what should you do?
explanation: Suffering for the Lord is a privilege, not a misfortune. When He appears, you will rejoice.
options: Be angry
Be sorrowful
Give up
Rejoice, because you partake of Christ's sufferings

content: 1 Peter 5:8 describes the devil as what?
explanation: Be sober and vigilant, for the enemy seeks to destroy.
options: A serpent
Darkness
A storm
A roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour

content: 1 Peter 2:2: As newborn babes, desire the spiritual ___ so that you may grow
explanation: God's Word is essential food for spiritual growth, like milk for newborns.
options: Water
Bread
Pure milk (the Word of God)
Meat

content: 1 Peter 3:3-4: Where does a woman's true beauty lie?
explanation: Inner (spiritual) beauty is more precious before God than outward appearance.
options: Jewelry
Hair
Clothing
The hidden person of the heart: a gentle and quiet spirit

content: 1 Peter 2:11 calls believers what on earth?
explanation: We are sojourners on earth; our true homeland is in heaven.
options: Owners
Citizens
Strangers and pilgrims (passing through)
Judges

content: 1 Peter 5:10: After you have suffered a while, what will the Lord do for you?
explanation: Difficulties are temporary. The Lord will restore, establish, and strengthen us.
options: Abandon you
Change you
Punish you more
Perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you on a firm foundation

content: 1 Peter 1:8 says believers love Christ even though they have never done what?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:8 — 'Though you have not seen him, you love him. Though you do not now see him, you believe in him and rejoice with joy that is inexpressible and filled with glory.'
options: Heard His name
Seen Him
Read about Him
Prayed to Him

content: What command of God does 1 Peter 1:15-16 quote?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:16 — 'Since it is written, You shall be holy, for I am holy.' This quotes Leviticus 11:44-45.
options: Be holy, for I am holy
Love your neighbor
Keep the Sabbath
Go and make disciples

content: According to 1 Peter 1:18-19, with what were the believers ransomed?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:18-19 — 'You were ransomed... not with perishable things such as silver or gold... but with the precious blood of Christ, like that of a lamb without blemish or spot.'
options: With gold and silver
By works of the fathers
With the precious blood of Christ
By Old Testament sacrifices

content: 1 Peter 1:24-25 says grass withers and flowers fall, but what remains forever?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:25 — 'But the word of the Lord remains forever. And this word is the good news that was preached to you.'
options: The word of the Lord
Solomon's temple
The kingdoms of the kings
The capital Jerusalem

content: What does 1 Peter 2:5 describe believers as?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:5 — 'You yourselves like living stones are being built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God.'
options: Lost sheep
Living stones being built into a spiritual house
Newly sown seed
Freed prisoners

content: What did Christ leave for us, according to 1 Peter 2:21?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:21 — 'For to this you have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you might follow in his steps.'
options: A book of laws
An example to follow
An inheritance
A spiritual army

content: How many times does 1 Peter 3:18 say Christ suffered for sins?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:18 — 'For Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring us to God.'
options: Yearly like Old Testament sacrifices
Each time the people sinned
Once for all
Seven times as a perfect number

content: Under what does 1 Peter 5:5-6 urge believers to humble themselves?
explanation: 1 Peter 5:6 — 'Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you.'
options: The mighty hand of God
The Mosaic law
The church elders
The power of the king

content: What does 1 Peter 5:7 urge believers to cast on God?
explanation: 1 Peter 5:7 — 'Casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.'
options: All possessions
All anxieties
All plans
All successes

content: According to 1 Peter 1:13, what should believers be sober about and set their full hope on?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:13 — 'Therefore, preparing your minds for action, and being sober-minded, set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.' 'GIRDING UP THE LOINS OF YOUR MINDS' (anazōsamenoi tas osphyas) = preparing the mind for serious action — like ancient Jews tying up long robes to run. The goal = the future GRACE on the day Christ is revealed.
options: On their strength (as described in the passage)
On wealth (per the textual narrative)
On the GRACE that will be brought when Jesus Christ is REVEALED
On ancestors (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 1:9, what is the outcome of believers' faith?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:9 — 'obtaining the outcome of your faith, the salvation of your souls.' 'TELOS' (outcome/end) = the destination of faith. NOT physical promises — but SALVATION OF SOUL. Unlike 'prosperity gospel' — eternal salvation, not material benefit.
options: Eternal wealth
The SALVATION of your SOULS
Visible miracles
Long life on earth

content: According to 1 Peter 1:10-12, what did OT prophets do about this salvation, and who illumined them?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:10-12 — 'Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace that was to be yours searched and inquired carefully, inquiring what person or time the Spirit of Christ in them was indicating when he predicted the sufferings of Christ and the subsequent glories... things into which angels long to look.' 3 groups involved: OT PROPHETS + new BELIEVERS + ANGELS.
options: Believed and passed on — through the church (as described in the passage)
Wrote it down — through the king (according to the text's framing)
Taught it — through Paul (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
SEARCHED and INQUIRED — the Spirit of Christ in them PREDICTED the sufferings + subsequent glories of Christ — even ANGELS LONG TO LOOK INTO

content: According to 1 Peter 1:14, believers are 'OBEDIENT CHILDREN' — must NOT conform to what?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:14 — 'As obedient children, do not be conformed to the passions of your former ignorance.' 'PASSIONS' (epithymiais) before Christ. Believers = NEW CHILDREN — cannot live by the OLD father (flesh). Next verse (1:15) — 'be holy' answers the call to forsake old desires.
options: To the FUTILE PASSIONS of your former IGNORANCE (before knowing God)
To the learned (according to the text's framing)
To the ancestors' example (as described in the passage)
To customs (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 Peter 1:17, since believers call God Father — the IMPARTIAL JUDGE — what must they do during their sojourn on earth?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:17 — 'And if you call on him as Father who judges impartially according to each one's deeds, conduct yourselves with fear throughout the time of your exile.' Two sides: FATHER (intimate) + JUDGE (reverent fear). 'EXILE' (paroikias) = sojourner — believers are strangers on earth, citizens of heaven.
options: Rest enjoying life
Accumulate wealth
Live IN FEAR (reverence)
Join politics

content: According to 1 Peter 1:23, by what SEED have believers been REBORN?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:23 — 'since you have been born again, not of perishable seed but of imperishable, through the living and abiding word of God.' 'SEED' (sporas) = a seed. New life = through IMPERISHABLE seed (the Word) — not through genetics. Contrast Adam (perishable seed) vs Christ (eternal seed).
options: By human seed (per the textual narrative)
By Israel's seed (according to the text's framing)
By the seed of faith (as described in the passage)
By IMPERISHABLE seed — through the LIVING and ABIDING WORD of God

content: According to 1 Peter 2:1, what must believers PUT AWAY to be like babies seeking PURE MILK of the Word?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:1 — 'So put away all malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander.' 5 things to PUT AWAY (apothemenoi): malice (kakian) + deceit (dolon) + hypocrisy (hypokriseis) + envy (phthonous) + slander (katalalias). Especially RELATIONAL — they block GROWTH (2:2 milk of the Word).
options: Sin generally (as described in the passage)
All MALICE + DECEIT + HYPOCRISY + ENVY + SLANDER
All thoughts (per the textual narrative)
All weakness (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:6 (citing Isaiah 28:16), what did God lay in Zion?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:6 — 'For it stands in Scripture: "Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone, a cornerstone chosen and precious, and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame."' Citing Isaiah 28:16. Peter links Christ = the CORNERSTONE. Next verse (2:7) — to believers = precious; to unbelievers = a stumbling stone (citing Ps 118:22 + Isa 8:14).
options: A precious CORNERSTONE, chosen — whoever BELIEVES in him will not be put to shame
A great temple (as described in the passage)
A priest (according to the text's framing)
A king (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:7-8, the stone REJECTED by the builders has become what?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:7-8 — 'So the honor is for you who believe, but for those who do not believe, "The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone," and "A stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense."' Citing Ps 118:22 + Isa 8:14. Christ is NOT neutral — DUAL EFFECT: precious stone (faith) or stumbling stone (unbelief).
options: Lost (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Crushed (as described in the passage)
Become the CHIEF CORNERSTONE — but to unbelievers a STUMBLING STONE, a ROCK of OFFENSE
Buried (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:10 (citing Hosea), what were believers (Gentiles) before? What are they now?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:10 — 'Once you were not a people, but now you are God's people; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.' Citing Hos 1:9-10, 2:23. The prophecy of Israel's REJECTION + RESTORATION now applies to the Gentile CHURCH being ADMITTED. Paul also uses this verse (Rom 9:25-26).
options: Before good — Now bad (per the textual narrative)
Before NOT a people — Now GOD'S PEOPLE; before NOT receiving MERCY — Now have RECEIVED MERCY
Before Jewish — Now Gentile (as described in the passage)
Before free — Now slaves (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:12, how should believers conduct themselves among GENTILES? Purpose?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:12 — 'Keep your conduct among the Gentiles honorable, so that when they speak against you as evildoers, they may see your good deeds and glorify God on the day of visitation.' Same teaching as Mt 5:16 (let your light shine). 'DAY OF VISITATION' = the day God brings grace (episkeptomai) — opponents may be saved through believers' testimony.
options: Live hidden — no need to show (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Argue often — to prove truth (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Avoid Gentiles — avoid uncleanness (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
Keep your conduct GOOD among the Gentiles — so that when they SPEAK AGAINST you as evildoers, they may see your GOOD DEEDS and GLORIFY God on the DAY of VISITATION

content: According to 1 Peter 2:17, what are the 4 commands?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:17 — 'Honor everyone. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the emperor.' 4 commands in 2 pairs: EVERYONE (general) - BROTHERHOOD (specific); GOD (FEAR — phobos) - EMPEROR (HONOR — timate). The point: fear of God EXCEEDS honor of the emperor. In Roman context, calling for submission to the Emperor.
options: HONOR everyone — LOVE the brotherhood — FEAR God — HONOR the emperor
Fear king — fear priest — fear ancestors — fear man
Love enemies — love wife — love children — love self
Praise God — praise king — praise self — praise brothers

content: According to 1 Peter 2:24, what did Christ do with our sins on the TREE (cross)? Purpose? By his WOUNDS we receive what?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:24 — 'He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. By his wounds you have been healed.' Citing Isaiah 53:5. 'TREE' (xylon) = cross (cf. Deut 21:23 'cursed'). 'HEALED' is primarily spiritual = forgiveness — not bodily healing (though possible).
options: Forgot our sins — so we remember (as described in the passage)
Hid sin — so we cover (per the textual narrative)
BORE our sins in his body on the TREE — so we might DIE to sin + LIVE to righteousness; by his WOUNDS we are HEALED
Simply forgave — so we rejoice (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:25, what were believers like before? To whom have they returned?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:25 — 'For you were straying like sheep, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.' Citing Isaiah 53:6 ('All we like sheep have gone astray'). Two titles for Christ: 'Shepherd' (poimēn) + 'Overseer' (episkopos = guardian). The letter's opening had Peter call himself an APOSTLE = overseer; now Christ is the TRUE overseer.
options: Lost like tourists — returned to a guide
Lost like SHEEP — have returned to the SHEPHERD + OVERSEER of your souls
Lost like a child — to mother
Lost like a blind man — to one who sees

content: According to 1 Peter 3:1-2, what must wives with UNBELIEVING husbands do to WIN their husbands without WORDS?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:1-2 — 'Likewise, wives, be subject to your own husbands, so that even if some do not obey the word, they may be won without a word by the conduct of their wives, when they see your respectful and pure conduct.' Peter does NOT counsel separation — calls for patience + good witness. CONDUCT speaks louder than WORDS.
options: Submit — husband may be WON OVER by the wife's PURE + RESPECTFUL CONDUCT — without words
Preach to husband daily (per the textual narrative)
Leave husband to keep faith (as described in the passage)
Force husband to church (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 3:7, how must husbands treat wives? Why?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:7 — 'Likewise, husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way, showing honor to the woman as the weaker vessel, since they are heirs with you of the grace of life, so that your prayers may not be hindered.' 'WEAKER VESSEL' (asthenesterō) = physically (not spiritually). 'CO-HEIRS' = spiritual equals. Mistreating one's wife → prayers HINDERED.
options: As servants — for discipline (as described in the passage)
As equals — for peace (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
As lower — as culture (according to the text's framing)
Live with them in an UNDERSTANDING way — as the WEAKER vessel — HONOR them (as fellow heirs of life's grace) — so PRAYERS not be HINDERED

content: According to 1 Peter 3:9, what must believers NOT do when wronged? What instead?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:9 — 'Do not repay evil for evil or reviling for reviling, but on the contrary, bless, for to this you were called, that you may obtain a blessing.' Same teaching as Jesus (Mt 5:44, Lk 6:28). Believers have NO right of 'eye for an eye' — calling is to BLESS. Why? BECAUSE we ourselves have RECEIVED blessing from God.
options: Take revenge fairly (according to the text's framing)
Stay silent — no reaction (as described in the passage)
Do NOT REPAY EVIL with EVIL, REVILING with REVILING — instead BLESS, since to this you were called to OBTAIN a blessing
Avoid — hide (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to 1 Peter 3:13-14, who would HARM believers when they are GOOD? If they suffer for righteousness, what?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:13-14 — 'Now who is there to harm you if you are zealous for what is good? But even if you should suffer for righteousness' sake, you will be blessed. Have no fear of them, nor be troubled.' Citing Isaiah 8:12-13. The point: GOODNESS usually earns praise — BUT if still persecuted, you PARTICIPATE with CHRIST in suffering + receive GREAT BLESSING.
options: Everyone — naturally hated (per the textual narrative)
Anyone might — if you suffer for RIGHTEOUSNESS you are BLESSED; do not fear what they fear, nor be troubled
Only enemies — be on guard (according to the text's framing)
No one — guaranteed safety (as described in the passage)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:7, the end of all things is AT HAND — so what must believers do?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:7 — 'The end of all things is at hand; therefore be self-controlled and sober-minded for the sake of your prayers.' 'END' (telos) is near — not a specific date but the PRINCIPLE: believers live IN EXPECTATION of Christ's return. 2 commands: SELF-CONTROLLED (sōphroneō) + SOBER (nēphō) — for the sake of prayer.
options: Cry warnings (according to the text's framing)
Hoard food (per the textual narrative)
Separate from society (as described in the passage)
Be SELF-CONTROLLED and SOBER-MINDED for the sake of PRAYER

content: According to 1 Peter 1:20, when was Christ FOREKNOWN, and when REVEALED?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:20 — 'He was foreknown before the foundation of the world but was made manifest in the last times for the sake of you.' 2 KEY words: 'FOREKNOWN' (proegnōsmenou) before the FOUNDATION + 'MANIFESTED' (phanerōthentos) in the LAST TIMES. Christ is NOT plan B after Adam fell — but the MAIN PLAN from before. Same idea as Rev 13:8.
options: FOREKNOWN before the foundation of the world — REVEALED in the LAST TIMES for our sake
Chosen at creation — revealed right after Adam
Chosen at Abraham — revealed through Moses (according to the text's framing)
Chosen at David — revealed at Bethlehem (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:4-5, Christ is the LIVING STONE rejected by men — believers are built into what?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:4-5 — 'As you come to him, a living stone rejected by men but in the sight of God chosen and precious, you yourselves like living stones are being built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.' EACH believer = LIVING STONE; community = TEMPLE; all = PRIESTS. Unlike OT (only Levites). The 'priesthood of all believers' concept.
options: A free nation (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
A SPIRITUAL HOUSE — a HOLY PRIESTHOOD — to offer SPIRITUAL SACRIFICES acceptable to God through Jesus Christ
A spiritual army (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
A religious council (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)

content: According to 1 Peter 2:13-14, whom must believers SUBMIT to? Why?
explanation: 1 Peter 2:13-15 — 'Be subject for the Lord's sake to every human institution, whether to the emperor as supreme... For this is the will of God, that by doing good you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish people.' Peter wrote UNDER Nero — yet taught submission. Reason NOT that the king is worthy — FOR THE LORD'S SAKE. Same idea Rom 13. Has a limit (Acts 5:29 'we must obey God rather than men').
options: Submit only to God — bypass government (as described in the passage)
Submit to good king — resist bad (according to the text's framing)
Submit to EVERY HUMAN INSTITUTION (emperor + governors) FOR THE LORD'S SAKE — for it is God's WILL: to silence the ignorant
Submit only to Jews (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to 1 Peter 3:19-20, to whom did Christ PREACH after his death? Linked to what era?
explanation: 1 Peter 3:19-20 — 'in which he went and proclaimed to the spirits in prison, because they formerly did not obey, when God's patience waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared, in which a few, that is, eight persons, were brought safely through water.' DIFFICULT verse — multiple interpretations: (a) Christ preached THROUGH Noah to that generation; (b) Christ proclaimed victory over evil spirits post-Resurrection. NOT explicit. Peter links the FLOOD + BAPTISM (3:21).
options: To Israel — patriarchs (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
To angels — at creation (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
To Gentiles — Abraham's time (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
By the Spirit, he went and PROCLAIMED to the SPIRITS IN PRISON — those who DISOBEYED in NOAH'S DAY when the ark was being prepared (8 saved through water)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:1, since Christ suffered in the flesh, what must believers ARM themselves with? What of those who suffer in flesh?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:1 — 'Since therefore Christ suffered in the flesh, arm yourselves with the same way of thinking, for whoever has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin.' 'ARM' (hoplisasthe) = equip. The point: suffering for faith = a DECISIVE BREAK with old sin. One who has suffered no longer finds sin attractive.
options: Arm yourselves with the SAME WAY of THINKING — for whoever has SUFFERED in the flesh has CEASED from SIN
Take a sacrifice — for forgiveness (as described in the passage)
Take power — to fight (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Take the law — for guidance (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:6, to whom was the GOSPEL preached? Purpose?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:6 — 'For this is why the gospel was preached even to those who are dead, that though judged in the flesh the way people are, they might live in the spirit the way God does.' DIFFICULT — 2 main interpretations: (a) believers who have died = heard the gospel while alive; (b) Christ preached the gospel to the dead. NOT explicit. Linked to 3:19. The focus: result = LIVING by the Spirit.
options: To Jews — to return (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
To Gentiles — to believe (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Even to the DEAD — that though judged in the flesh the way people are, they might LIVE in the spirit the way God does
To angels — to learn (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:10-11, what must each believer do with their GIFT? Who speaks must speak how?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:10-11 — 'As each has received a gift, use it to serve one another, as good stewards of God's varied grace: whoever speaks, as one who speaks oracles of God; whoever serves, as one who serves by the strength that God supplies.' 'STEWARDS' (oikonomoi). 'ORACLES OF GOD' (logia theou).
options: Keep it for self-edification (as described in the passage) (per the original content) (per the textual narrative)
Boast for honor (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Sell for profit (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
USE it to SERVE ONE ANOTHER as good STEWARDS of God's varied grace — Whoever SPEAKS, as one who speaks ORACLES OF GOD; whoever SERVES, as one serving by the strength GOD SUPPLIES

content: According to 1 Peter 4:14, those INSULTED for Christ's name have what?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:14 — 'If you are insulted for the name of Christ, you are blessed, because the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you.' 'SPIRIT OF GLORY' (to tēs doxēs Pneuma) = the Holy Spirit. Image: as God's Spirit RESTED on the OT tabernacle (Ex 40:34) → now the Spirit RESTS on suffering believers. Same teaching Mt 5:11-12.
options: Disgrace — to be discreet (as described in the passage)
BLESSED — for the SPIRIT of GLORY, the Spirit of God, RESTS upon them
Peace — no reaction (according to the text's framing)
Reason to resign (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:17, where does judgment BEGIN? What then becomes of those NOT obeying the gospel?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:17 — 'For it is time for judgment to begin at the household of God; and if it begins with us, what will be the outcome for those who do not obey the gospel of God?' 'HOUSEHOLD OF GOD' = the Church. Argument from greater to lesser: if the Father's CHILDREN are tested by fire, what of NON-children? Same idea as Ezek 9:6 (judgment begins at the temple).
options: From the HOUSEHOLD of GOD — so what will become of those who do not obey the gospel of God?
From Israel — Gentiles forgiven (according to the text's framing)
From Rome — the Church spared (per the textual narrative)
From appointed days — cannot guess (as described in the passage)

content: According to 1 Peter 4:19, those who suffer ACCORDING TO GOD'S WILL must do what with their souls?
explanation: 1 Peter 4:19 — 'Therefore let those who suffer according to God's will entrust their souls to a faithful Creator while doing good.' 'ENTRUST' (paratithesthōsan) = commit something to someone for safekeeping. Same word Lk 23:46 'Father, into your hands I commit my spirit'. 'FAITHFUL CREATOR' (pistos ktistēs) = UNIQUE label in NT. The God who created you — knows how to keep you.
options: Plead for deliverance — every way
Cope with circumstances — by planning
ENTRUST their souls to a FAITHFUL CREATOR — while doing GOOD
Complain to the church (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 Peter 5:1-4, how should Peter's ELDERS shepherd God's flock, and what reward when the CHIEF SHEPHERD appears?
explanation: 1 Peter 5:2-4 — 'shepherd the flock of God that is among you, exercising oversight, not under compulsion, but willingly, as God would have you; not for shameful gain, but eagerly; not domineering over those in your charge, but being examples to the flock. And when the chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.' 'CHIEF SHEPHERD' (archipoimēn) — UNIQUE in NT. Distinct from Heb 13:20 (GREAT Shepherd), John 10 (GOOD Shepherd).
options: Shepherd for glory — much money (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
Shepherd for authority — high office (as described in the passage) (per the original content) (per the textual narrative)
Shepherd by tradition — esteem (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Shepherd NOT under compulsion but WILLINGLY — NOT for shameful gain but eagerly — NOT domineering but BEING EXAMPLES; when the CHIEF SHEPHERD appears you will receive the UNFADING CROWN of glory
